title: relbunium involucratum
intro: Relbunium involucratum, also known as the "Singapore orchid tree," is a flowering plant in the bignonia family native to Malaysia and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it's used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Relbunium involucratum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
